text: Fill in the blanks about the arm and cubital fossa.
questions: The arm is divided into anterior and posterior compartments by the medial and lateral *intermuscular* septa.
The anterior compartment of the arm contains three muscles: *biceps* brachii, *brachialis*, and coracobrachialis.
Saturday night paralysis is caused by compression of the *radial* nerve as it runs in the *spiral groove/radial groove* of the humerus.
The boundaries of the cubital fossa are: laterally the *brachioradialis*, medially the *pronator teres*, and superiorly an imaginary line between the epicondyles.
The median cubital vein connects the *cephalic* and *basilic* veins and is the preferred site for venipuncture.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
